skill.name: nano-banana-2
skill.description: Generate images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview (Nano Banana 2) via inference.sh CLI. Capabilities: text-to-image, image editing, multi-image input (up to 14 images), Google Search grounding. Triggers: nano banana 2, nanobanana 2, gemini 3.1 flash image, gemini 3 1 flash image preview, google image generation
skill.summary: Generate images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview (Nano Banana 2) via inference.sh CLI. Cap...
skill.author: inference-shell

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The 32 external_command detections are bash command examples in markdown documentation (not actual code execution). The 5 network detections are legitimate documentation URLs (inference.sh, example.com). The 6 weak_crypto detections are scanner misidentifications of table formatting - no cryptographic code exists in this skill. This is a legitimate image generation API wrapper skill using inference.sh CLI.

low_findings.title: External Command Examples in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected shell commands in markdown documentation. These are user-facing examples showing CLI usage, not actual code execution. The skill uses inference.sh CLI (infsh) as a legitimate API wrapper for Google Gemini image generation.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Detected
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged hardcoded URLs. These are legitimate documentation and reference links (inference.sh domain, example.com placeholder). No unauthorized network requests or credential exfiltration.

content.user_title: Generate Images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash
content.value_statement: Create stunning AI-generated images using Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview model. This skill enables text-to-image generation, image editing, and multi-image composition through a simple CLI interface via inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview
Edit existing images by providing input images with modification instructions
Generate multiple images (up to 4) from a single prompt
Ground outputs with real-time Google Search data when enabled
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and authentication
Maximum 14 input images for multi-image editing operations
Output quality depends on prompt clarity and specificity
API usage may incur costs based on inference.sh pricing

use_cases.title: Marketing Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate custom images for social media posts, blog articles, and marketing materials without design expertise.
use_cases.target_user: Content marketers and social media managers

use_cases.title: Concept Visualization
use_cases.description: Rapidly visualize product ideas, architectural concepts, or creative directions before committing to production.

use_cases.title: Image Enhancement and Editing
use_cases.description: Modify existing images by adding elements, changing styles, or enhancing specific features using natural language.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a serene mountain landscape at sunrise with misty valleys and golden light reflecting off snow peaks.

prompt_templates.title: Styled Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a photorealistic portrait of a futuristic robot chef in a modern kitchen, studio lighting, shallow depth of field, 85mm lens.

prompt_templates.title: Image Editing with Reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Transform this daytime photo into a nighttime scene with stars in the sky, moonlight casting shadows, and warm interior lights visible through windows.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Image Composition
prompt_templates.prompt: Combine these product photos into a cohesive lifestyle scene showing the items in use at a modern workspace with natural lighting.

output_examples.output: Returns generated image(s) in the response with metadata including resolution, aspect ratio, and generation parameters.

output_examples.input: Add a rainbow to this landscape photo with storm clouds clearing
output_examples.output: Returns edited image showing the original landscape with added rainbow effect and modified sky elements.

best_practices: Use specific, descriptive prompts with style, lighting, and composition details for better results
Start with lower resolution (1K) for testing prompts before generating final 4K outputs
Provide reference images when editing to ensure the AI understands the base content
anti_patterns: Avoid vague prompts like 'make it pretty' without specific visual direction
Do not upload sensitive or confidential images for processing
Avoid requesting copyrighted character likenesses or trademarked content

faq.question: What is the inference.sh CLI and how do I install it?
faq.answer: inference.sh is a platform for running AI applications. Install via: npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools. Authentication is required via infsh login.

faq.question: How many images can I generate in one request?
faq.answer: Use the num_images parameter to generate up to 4 images from a single prompt in one request.

faq.question: Can I edit existing images with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, provide input image URLs in the images array along with editing instructions in the prompt parameter.

faq.question: What image formats are supported for input?
faq.answer: Input images support JPEG, PNG, and WebP formats. You can provide up to 14 images for multi-image operations.

faq.question: How does Google Search grounding work?
faq.answer: Enable google_search parameter to let the model access real-time information for accurate depictions of current events, weather, or news.

faq.question: What resolutions are available for output?
faq.answer: Choose from 1K (default), 2K, or 4K resolution. Higher resolutions require more processing time and may have higher costs.
